candidates.title: Travel-Themed T-Shirt Design
candidates.tools: Photoshop · Illustrator — for print-ready CMYK output and scalable vector artwork
candidates.brief: I’m building a line of T-shirts that capture the spirit of modern travellers—pieces someone can wear whether they are crossing continents or exploring their own backyard. I need a creative apparel designer who can translate that wanderlust into standout graphics and compelling typography, sized and pre-flighted for screen print or DTG. The brief • Style: distinctly “traveller,” not strictly casual, formal, or athletic. Think versatile silhouettes, minimal but expressive artwork, and colours that work internationally as well as domestically. • Destinations: designs should resonate with both domestic and international journeys, so references can be universal icons (passports, coordinates, skylines) rather than location-specific clichés. • Format: layered vector files (AI or EPS) plus high-resolution PNGs with transparent backgrounds. Please keep text editable. • Printing specs: provide CMY

candidates.title: Vector Music Notation Diagrams
candidates.brief: *****Automated responses will be rejected without review. This is the first of eight progressively challenging engagements spanning the next two months — each requiring a demonstrated understanding of music theory. If your knowledge is genuine, these tasks will be straightforward. If it is not, you will be unable to complete them.***** I have a set of 21 small diagrams that explain entry-level music-theory ideas—staves, clefs, notes, rests, accidentals, scales, time signatures and a few Italian tempo markings. They will sit inside a dark UI (#111119), so every line, stem and glyph must render crisply in light colours on that background. The finished artwork must be supplied exactly as optimised SVG 1.1 files, one per diagram, with: • viewBox only (0 0 600 300 unless I note otherwise) • no hard-coded width/height, transparent background, ~24 px safe margin • all text converted to outlines

candidates.title: Horticulture Company Profile With Photos
candidates.tools: Illustrator · InDesign — for print-ready CMYK output and layered/retouched assets
candidates.brief: I need a fresh, visually engaging company profile that I can send straight to the printer and hand out at trade shows or client meetings. Its primary job is marketing, so every page has to sell our horticulture expertise at a glance. Content • Company history and mission – a concise narrative that highlights our roots, values and long-term vision in sustainable horticulture. • Services offered – clear, persuasive copy that outlines greenhouse construction, crop consultation and turnkey project management. Imagery High-resolution, royalty-free photos are essential. Where possible, weave in shots of our nurseries, equipment in action and finished landscape projects so the reader feels the scale and quality of our work. Design & Format Print-ready, CMYK PDF (and the working file in InDesign or Illustrator) at 300 dpi, A4 size with a 3 mm bleed. Clean typography, plenty of white space and co

candidates.title: Logo Delivery via PM Tool
candidates.brief: I already have the logo designed; what I need now is a fool-proof way to let the requester view it inside a project-management environment rather than sending files back and forth. My preference is to upload it directly to a project management tool so everything stays organised and versioned. Here’s the help I’m after: • Recommend (or confirm) the most straightforward PM platform for this single-asset hand-off. • Set up a space, board, or project with the right permissions so the client can log in and view/download the logo without friction. • Show me exactly where and how to upload the file, add any preview or description text, and enable comments for feedback. • Provide a brief, repeatable checklist so I can do the same for future design deliveries. If you can walk me through the steps on a live screen-share or supply a concise written guide with screenshots, even better. The end goal

candidates.title: Design & Build a Customer Reviews and Testimonials Page for Broadband Website
candidates.tools: Premiere Pro · InDesign · Illustrator — for multi-page layout and video/motion editing
candidates.brief: Reviews & Customer Stories Page Design & Build About Us Gigabit IQ is a UK-based full-fibre broadband provider focused on delivering fast, reliable internet alongside industry-leading online safety and cybersecurity features for families. Website: www.gigabitiq.com Project Overview We are looking for an experienced web designer/developer to create a dedicated Reviews & Customer Stories page for our website. The goal is to build trust, showcase customer experiences, and help convert visitors into customers through a combination of review platform ratings, written testimonials, and short customer video stories. The page should feel modern, trustworthy, family-friendly, and aligned with the Gigabit IQ brand. candidates.title: Complete Shopify Store Design
candidates.tools: InDesign · Illustrator — for layered/retouched assets and multi-page layout
candidates.brief: My Shopify catalogue is already uploaded and the photography is in place; what’s missing is a polished, modern-and-sleek interface that makes the store feel truly finished. I want the theme refined and the layout tightened up so every interaction feels effortless on desktop and mobile alike. Scope of work • Home page – build a strong hero, integrate a dynamic Featured Products section, and surface real Customer Reviews for social proof. • Product pages – clean typography, crisp image galleries, intuitive variant selection, and a persuasive, mobile-friendly add-to-cart flow. • Checkout page – style the experience so it stays on brand right through to payment, keeping performance and conversion best-practices in mind.
